description_long: This house features a distinctive V-shaped layout with two wings extending from a central section, all topped by a prominent red hip roof. The exterior walls are finished in white, with large rectangular windows arranged in neat rows across both stories, providing ample natural light. The central section includes a covered entryway supported by columns, adding a touch of formality and symmetry to the facade. The roof is multi-tiered, with the main hip roof intersected by smaller hips over each wing, creating architectural interest. The house appears to be constructed primarily from concrete or masonry, with glass windows and metal or wood framing. The overall design suggests a modern adaptation of traditional residential forms, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A large, two-story V-shaped house with a red hip roof, white exterior walls, and rows of large rectangular windows. The central entryway features columns and a covered porch, giving the house a formal and symmetrical appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central entryway, columns, and the V-shaped layout with both wings visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of one wing, the multi-tiered hip roof, and the window rows along the facade.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full V-shape, the central covered porch, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other wing, roof structure, and consistent window placement.
attribute_entryway_design: Central covered entryway with columns, steps, and a small porch
